name: St. Louis Heights
desc: Ridge community above Kaimuki — sweeping views, quiet streets, family-oriented, but steep and winding roads.
insider: The view from the top of St. Louis Drive rivals any paid lookout on the island — Diamond Head, ocean, and Waikiki skyline
Wa'ahila Ridge Trail starts at the top of Ruth Place and is one of the best uncrowded ridge hikes with Manoa Valley views
Streets are narrow and steep — 4WD or at least a confident driver recommended, and parallel parking is an art form
Saint Louis School area has the best trick-or-treating on Oahu — locals drive from all over for Halloween
Chaminade University campus at the base has a nice quiet quad for walking and the library is open to the public
The higher you go, the cooler and breezier it gets — upper St. Louis Heights can be 5-8 degrees cooler than Kaimuki below
No commercial businesses exist in St. Louis Heights — you're driving down to Kaimuki or Kapahulu for everything
The sunrise over Maunalua Bay seen from the upper streets is free and rivals any paid tour experience
Street parking is extremely limited — most homes have steep driveways that are tricky to navigate
Robert Louis Stevenson is said to have visited the area in the 1880s — the 'St. Louis' name has older Catholic mission roots
